evidence: "A film's story is always the final arbiter for a successful film but acting, directing and production values must provide equal measure for that overall success to transcend."
"The Martian builds on that philosophy and does an excellent job of describing how both the one left behind and the rescuers go about affecting the rescue..."
"The story bears the largest responsibility in The Sea of Trees... the audience is provided with the rationale and the necessary back-story..."
"The screenplay writer lifts the premise of seeing ghosts directly from The Sixth Sense... misses the mark entirely."
"The story stayed believable... even with all the cards stacked against him, Matt comes home (in one piece)!"
"The audience is drawn into the story to such an extent that individual scenes flow, one to the next, in a seamless movement..."
"Writing, acting, directing and production are first class and set a very high standard indeed!"
"The production values of the film were great with nearly seamless crossing between real and CGI."
"I make it a habit to re-watch the 'Unforgiven' around once a year for the purpose of rekindling my ability to 'smell' the rain."
"Love Naomi Watts' film work. She provides a lot more than simple female lead creds she carries her films."
"I attribute it all to his ability to make me 'smell' the rain throughout the piece."
"The audience can nod and share the main character's sense of peace."
"I will remember and cherish this story and this film for the rest of my life."
"The film addressed and portrayed this series of events that each and everyone of us will face in some fashion as we age into the future."

summary: The user has a strong preference for complex dramas with high production quality and emotional depth. They appreciate slow-paced storytelling that allows for character development and narrative intricacies, often resonating with the emotional themes presented in the films.
